title: Container Threat Models, Users, Capabilities, Seccomp, Rootless, and Daemon Authority · Stakeholder Container Mission
summary: A legal-aid document processing team. The team needs to process hostile files with non-root identity, read-only runtime, dropped capabilities, bounded resources, isolated mounts and network, and explicit remaining sandbox limitations. Planned artifact: Deliver Least-Privilege Document Processing Boundary with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.
Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.
Retain or tighten seccomp, AppArmor or SELinux, no-new-privileges, read-only roots, masked paths, and writable tmpfs exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel
steps.instruction:
project case — A project document parser runs privileged as root, mounts daemon authority, writes the image filesystem, has network access, and no resource caps. Remove authority and state the remaining shared-kernel risk.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-rootless-userns-daemon-socket-1743fi". Tailor its security-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket into observable performance evidence. content.code:
# Evidence: docker-rootless-userns-daemon-socket-1743fi
  - competency: docker-rootless-userns-daemon-socket
    artifact: security-policy.yaml
    decision: rootless userns daemon socket
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: production-like canary with read-only application state
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: 1743fi
    change: |
      user: 10001:10001
      read_only: true
      cap_drop: [ALL]
      security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true]
      tmpfs: [/tmp]
      network_mode: none
    verify: docker inspect document-worker --format json
    failure: Reject privileged mode, daemon-socket mounting, broad devices, root-by-default, or a claim that containers form a hostile-code sandbox.
    repair: Remove daemon authority, choose rootless or user namespaces where applicable, drop capabilities, bound writable paths, and state residual kernel risk.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
